page: Amazon Web Services x2iedn.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by the Intel Xeon 8375C processor. It features 128 virtual CPUs representing 64 physical cores with a dedicated allocation running at 3.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 4096 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 32 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided by 3800 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and the system operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the x2iedn.32xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities, achieving top-tier results in Passmark CPU and encryption tests, as well as Geekbench ray tracing. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small and large block operations scoring in the top 25 percent. Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput are also strong. However, without dedicated GPU acceleration, large-scale LLM text generation performance remains weak, as shown by the bottom 25 percent score for 70B model text generation.

Given its massive memory-to-CPU ratio and high-speed local NVMe storage, this instance offers high resource density for memory-bound enterprise applications. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that require caching massive datasets in memory rather than relying on slower external storage. The server is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, high-throughput Redis deployments, heavy database operations, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services x2iedn.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 128 vCPUs running at 3.5 GHz, it features 4096 GB of DDR4 RAM and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in Passmark CPU and encryption tests, alongside strong memory bandwidth and Redis throughput. However, the lack of hardware accelerators limits its efficiency for large-scale LLM text generation. This server is highly suited for in-memory databases, large-scale Redis deployments, and data-intensive enterprise applications requiring massive memory capacity and high-speed local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x2iedn.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs, 4096 GB of DDR4 RAM, 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for enterprise databases.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x2iedn.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 128 vCPUs, 4 TB of DDR4 RAM, and 3.8 TB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized server with local NVMe storage and high-speed networking for massive in-memory databases and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors at 3.5 GHz
4096 GB of DDR4 memory providing 32 GB of RAM per core
3800 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier performance in CPU encryption and memory bandwidth benchmarks
Optimized for in-memory databases, Redis, and heavy database operations
